reviews.review_description: Sucks

reviews.review_title: Best execution ever for a VR game
reviews.review_description:
DISC has been one of my top favourites in VR lately. It's got the best gameplay, an awesome team of developers, and good updates.
The gameplay follows an old classic game you might recognise from RecRoom as "paddleball". There's the DISC, and your goal is to... get it in the other person's goal. There's a campaign mode where if you have no friends (just like me!) you can get better and better at the game. I love all of the different shot types too! And although the multiplayer is laggy - it's always fun to just throw a few discs at your friends (and win)!
The team of developers are awesome too, it's really easy to let them know of any issues that might come up, and with the funding from a big company like ASICS - they're always giving away free stuff for competitions and giveaways, and that's really rare for a billion dollar company.
For the updates, they literally released a new major update today haha. The arcade mode is awesome, reminds me of Beat Saber.

DISC is an awesome game, especially because it's Free to play.

Also - if you love Tron (excited for the Ares movie!), this is the game for you.
amazing game however i dont know if this was a glitch or if nobody was playing but i tried for a long time to play online and there was no one playing so it lacks challenge from other people

reviews.review_title: Loved the game when I first got it but
reviews.review_description: Now for some reason the game isn't registering my return or even letting me serve. The disk goes straight through. Awful
Needs the ability to change color of the disc for people with low vision

No matter what I do i cannot get the game to recognize my controller.  Ive gone into tutorial and can hit disc but it gets halfway back and dissappears. When i go into regular game it doesn't even let me hit disc. My hand just goes right through it

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback. Controllers should be fully recognized in DISC. Please try re-drawing your roomscale boundary (press the Meta button on your right controller). If the issue continues, reach us at disc_support@asics.com so we can help.

reviews.review_description: Game hitboxs is so bunz i hit it and it still goes in my goal idc if early game it still pmo. fix your game 🙄
I’m just sad that more people don’t play this game.😣
Every time I start the game it tells me to resize my room.  So I tried playing outside and it still says to resize.  I'm pretty disgusted and disappointed.  I was really looking forward to playing 😐

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing this. DISC won’t start unless a roomscale boundary is created, and you’ll need to stay within it during play. Please press the Meta button on your right controller to set or resize your boundary. If the issue continues, reach us at disc_support@asics.com so we can help.

reviews.review_title: The disc battle game I've waited for
reviews.review_description: This game has been awesome to test out as it was being developed. The company listened to the testers and took their advice. Something that few companies do. Disc is a great game for those that enjoyed Echo VR. You will enjoy this game. For those that are fans of Tron. You'll appreciate the nod to the movies. Overall amazing game to play solo or with friends. I would highly recommend Disc to anyone.

developer_response.body: Thanks for sharing this. The disc is meant to respond to your hand movements. Please try re-drawing your roomscale boundary (press the Meta button on your right controller). If it still doesn’t work, contact us at disc_support@asics.com so we can assist.

developer_response.body:
We understand your frustration, and we appreciate you letting us know about the issue. It took some time for our dev team to track it down, but the problem has now been resolved.

We’d love for you to update the game and give it another try — everything should run smoothly now. If you enjoy the improved experience, we’d be grateful if you could reflect that in an updated review 🙂

reviews.review_description: I have only played the game for about an hour and a half. I am upto.boss number 5 and they were very easy to get up to. I like the moves in the game. I wish there were more and I th8nk there needs to be a move that will allow me to slow the disc down. So fun to play! It’s like a full-body air hockey in awesome locations and against different bosses. 
Addicted 🔥
This is my new favorite VR game. Not only is it fast pace and exciting it's a great workout. I recommend this game to anyone that's looking to play a new sport and get a great workout.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the kind words! We are excited to push updates in the future that provide an even more engaging workout experience.

Please feel free to share DISC with friends who you think may be interested :)

reviews.review_description:
This Air-Hockey sim is amazing and a great workout too. If only I had the room space to move about freely, then I wouldn’t let many goals in, especially own goals lol.
I’m currently on Lvl3 (Boss Cat) and this cheeky kitty has some great moves within his paws.
Thoroughly enjoyable and I recommend it :)

Human air hockey where you hands and face are the disc smackers. When you got into the arena and look around at the crowd cheering you on in the different locales it really makes you feel like you are at a major sporting event! Disc and ASICS really have a cool experience here that if you don’t watch out you’ll be getting your fitness sweat on in no time. Still in early access though, so can’t wait to see what the future holds for Disc.

Right off the bat, the game's presentation is great. The menus are clean, the game's visuals leave nothing to be desired, and the sound design is fantastic. Everything from menu navigation to the actual gameplay feels snappy and responsive. 

But there are some glaring issues that I'm baffled made it out the door in a V1.0. Something I noticed immediately after getting into the gameplay, your in-game hands don't match up with the position of your controllers even remotely. Your hands are offset forward by 2-3 inches, and ends up being incredibly distracting. This could have been an attempt to add some reach assistance? But it just ended up causing a really bad disconnect in my hand-eye coordination in a game where you wouldn't want to mess with it. 
There is also no way, as far as I've been able to find, to continue to the next campaign mission directly after finishing one. You have two options; you either replay the mission you just did, or return to the main menu. It adds a lot of additional friction to the gamemode you'll be playing most often. 
The in-game hands lack any kind of capacitive touch gestures, which feels incredibly odd in 2025. Even just a combination of thumbs up and pointing would have been appreciated.
The lack of ANY kind of thumbstick movement was a pretty big downer. You are absolutely REQUIRED to have a larger than typical playspace in order to cover the entire width of your goal, let alone move edge to edge on your side of the court. Now granted, the desire for thumbstick movement is simply a matter of taste, and likely just makes this game not for me. 

It's clear that the developers put a lot of TLC into the development of this game, and I hope that trend continues into the future. I want to like this game a lot, and I hope it makes the moves necessary to succeed.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share such a detailed review — this kind of feedback does not go unnoticed.

We truly appreciate your acknowledgement of the work that’s gone into the game, and we hope that future updates can warrant an improved review. Either way, we look forward to seeing you in the experience and hope you enjoy what we’ve built — and what we’ll continue building!

reviews.review_title: Best game with a disc since echo
reviews.review_description: DISC reminds me of Echo and how competitive a game can get with just a few mechanics in it. But those mechanics can be used in a number of different ways to try and score on the opponent. The disc can be manipulated in so many different ways that you never know what your oppent is going to do until they actually do something. Also, the disc can travel at numerous speeds making it unpredictable at any speed.

reviews.review_title: Sucks doesn't work fix your glitches pls
reviews.review_description: I can't even hit the disc my hand goes through it.

developer_response.body:
Sorry for the frustration — this issue was reported to us recently, and while it took us a little time, we’ve now fixed it.

We’d love for you to update the game and give it another try. The experience should be much smoother now, and we’d really appreciate it if you could share your updated thoughts in a new review 🙂

reviews.review_description: The game mechanics are all there. The environment adds to immersion. Ive been looking at ways to improve my reaction and hand eye coordination and this is a great game to do that. It’s a great sport with a low barrier to entry but a high skill ceiling. Can’t wait for more features to come out.

The core experience of Disc is solid. Hitting the disc is solid and satisfying. The gameplay is simple yet can be complex, you can alter the way the disc flies down the court. The disc also can be incredibly fast, which makes for more challenging rallies. This simple yet deep style of gameplay is really good. 

There are only a few things that hold the game back from being the best version it could be. (Which can always be fixed in later updates, which the developers are very receptive to feedback.) The goals behind the players are just a bit too big, moving side to side can be challenging, especially in smaller playspaces. 
The current offerings of game modes are a bit sparse, you can play against bots (lvls 1-10) and against players. Both are really solid in their own right, but if you get bored of one mode, there's not much else you can do. There's a greyed-out option of arcade mode which sounds exciting, however I'm reviewing the game as what it currently offers, which arcade is not released as of yet.

To reiterate, this game has a solid core and is fun to play. It's definitely worth playing since it's free.

Imagine a game like Human AirHockey with a Disc. You can slap shots in goal by creating some spin or bashing it against the side walls of the arena. So far, you can play their featured 1v1 Gamemode Online and Offline vs bots! This game is still in its early stages and I can’t wait to see what it has to arrive later. This game feels like your playing an actual sport, it has proper scoring similar to other sports and a great feel when slamming the disc off your hand. Great game all in all and would 100% recommend. Im going to force my friends to play it with me! The game isn’t the only good thing to review upon as it also has a very kind and respectful community behind it and an active developer team that is working hard to fix bugs that are mentioned
and they listen to what the community wants. They put their community first and thats what many users look for in a game!

Developer & Community Discord: https://discord.gg/playdiscvr

Great game throughout the early development, the devs continue to add to the great feel of the game. Only played the bot so far, however that was a fun experience. Looking forward to playing people in the game when time allows. I can’t attest to the XR feel of the game as I only have a quest2, however the full VR arenas feel great, and the gameplay feels very intuitive and natural.

This game has come a long way from the beta stages and is very polished I enjoy the very high intense gameplay and how much you have to move compared to many other vr games and I’m really hoping for a competitive side for this game that’ll challenge everyone.
There is no lobby and i finish the game and there was nothing else to do
I dont know why people write low stars because the worst part of this game is the final boss bieng hard but thats kinda the point sooooo
💫💫  💫        FUN!FRESH!.SO GOOD   !!!     💫💫💫

developer_response.body: Thanks for letting us know. We are glad to hear you enjoy the game. Look out for future updates soon!

reviews.review_title: lets curve and spin the disc around
reviews.review_description:
go training in VR and smash the disc to the other side's goal with some bots to level up and then you are ready for a 1v1 with other pplayers to beat them all and level up in some great places.
more places to come for sure lets hope some Science Fiction places to and can't wait to see us playing in more modes like a 2v2 could be hard for the devs but fun for us ;-))
yep i need to say as player you need some extra space left and right because the goal on the back of you is a little bigger than expected or you will hit someone watching you so be careful too

I've never really settled on a game after Echo ended up shutting down, but this might just be the one. Matches feel immensely satisfying and there's definitely potential for a high skill ceiling. Be warned, it does require a bit of open space, but if that's not an issue I would highly recommend giving it a download.

reviews.review_title: Real potential for competitive vr scene
reviews.review_description: I’ve been involved with most of these types of games for a while and this has been every well executed so far. There are plenty of mechanics you can use to outsmart and outperform your opponents. The devs have listened a lot to the community so I think there is a good chance this game skyrockets in popularity and sees an influx of excited players.

I’ve been playing since the closed alpha and watching this game change and improve throughout its development has been great. They listen too feedback from players and really try to make it the best experience they can. I can’t wait to see how it moves forward with the potential it has.

reviews.review_title: Competitive game with well built featurs
reviews.review_description: This game offers both competitive and chill gameplay aspects. Me personally I’m more of a competitive player and this game is￼ offers a very nice competitive play option. Making this game fun for everyone. Not to mention the very trained and skilled development team to make sure the game stays this high quality. 10/10 would recommend ￼:)

reviews.review_title: The Perfect Successor to Echo VR
reviews.review_description: At the time of this review, DISC is a pretty new game, just combing out of the demo. However, I have faith that it will be a great game. At the moment there is not much to talk about, but I have a feeling this game could have a great comp scene like Echo (RIP) one day. Great idea with great developers.

Wow!  I wasn't sure what to expect, but the concept of human air hockey has completely won me over.  Even though it's just been bots for now, I love how increasingly difficult they become as you progress and can totally see this being a ton of fun against actual people.  Absolutely recommend it!
It's pretty good, I really like the mechanics. It's like echo and handball had a kid, and I Love it. Easy to. Play, and good to get a stretch in since you sometimes have to reach for it.
This game is the closest thing you'll get to our beloved Sparc. It's very well made and shares the same vibe. I beta tested this game and it's come a long way. It's definitely worth a try!

I think an important aspect of VR is competitiveness, and Disc brings that. This may be the only game I feel I can genuinely 1v1 with my friends in, where we can joke and test each other skills, and its a blast! I without a doubt recommend a download

This game is very fun and has lots of potential, obviously its a new game so it has some bugs and kinks that need to be worked out, but overall its very competitive with a high skill ceiling. Im very excited to continue playing and watch the game grow.

I played the beta when it was available and I did like it but had a lot of bugs to be worked around and now with the bugs being fixed I can say that I do enjoy the game more and would honestly recommend to people if they want to play an disc type game again.

Doesn’t quite live up to the echo VR experienced, but definitely has potential! I had the chance to test this game in closed EA, it had a few things that needed work on but I think for what it is, it will be a good fun game to play.
I didn't come into this game with too much expectations. But it blew me away! The simple yet fun mechanics are a blast to play with, and I can't wait to play this with all my mates tommorow!
Haven't tried playing person but computer is hard enough to be fun.quite a workout fast paced.
This is so much fun to play!  Well done.

developer_response.body: We are happy to hear this DeeDee. Hope you enjoy our future updates!

I like the air hockey elements in a 3d space and the stationary gameplay. The different types of shots are crazy too.

As of release, the content is limited, but what is out so far is fairly polished and quite fun!
This is a very fun and challenging game that I recommend everyone tries.
Honestly a great game, the development brings me back to echo vr days

Solidly made with strong physics.
I like the aspect of it.
Great game to play with a buddy.
Kinda reminds me of curve ball 3D from cool math games
Best game ever

The only change is like I stated, the ability to decrease the length of the goal, especially if your opponent agrees to it

developer_response.body: Hi Buckeye, we thank you for downloading and giving DISC a shot. Unfortunate to hear the goal area was too large for your play space. We will look into finding a solution to this problem. Hope you enjoy the rest of the experience!

reviews.review_title: Try a new fun mechanic, it’s free
reviews.review_description: We don’t have a lot of room-scale games anymore, so I enjoy an easy to learn hard to master game like Disc. The disc can move really fast and there are launch modes which lead to strategy. 4 stars because it’s still very much a work in progress and I’m looking for more fun elements that mix up the gameplay.

I've been following DISC from pretty much the start. Having seen their dedication and love, I can proudly say it definitely has paid off for the initial release of DISC. packed with amazing sound design and some neat polished graphics that's accompanied by a detailed arena, followed up with unique physics, DISC delivers on a fun physical sport converted into a game that can almost be addictive to some !!

There are some flaws, though. The goal itself is very.. very large, as someone who lives in Europe with a medium to large sized room, I still am unable to reach from one end of the goal post to the other (used with roomscale boundary) making some shots unsaveable unless I sacrifice a limb ! lol. if the overall scale of the goals could be reduced by a small amount, I feel as if that would be optimal for all users who are not gifted with larger rooms. The DISC itself is also pretty hard to see. it could be that it's too thin or the ambient lighting around the disc is not bright enough.

However, the game is still a great play with tons of potential for possible upcoming features, major updates, and events. DISC on! 🩶
This game reminds me of the echo days and its mechanisms are so cool! 100% would recommend!

I’ve followed the development of DISC for a while and am definitely impressed with the final product, hoping for a competitive side of the game to start going strong as we need more sports style vr competition games
ASICS does it again. What an excellent app!!!  VR is the future and ASICS is taking a strong lead it making it all happen!  Definitely worth the download!!
Great fun, can be competitive and a good workout! Got my friends on this game too!
